zh.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Paulett於1185年之前與Erenburga de Paulett在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Paulett于1185年之前与Erenburga de Paulett在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Paulett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: nickname from Middle English pail(l)et ‘bed or mattress of straw, pallet’, sometimes for someone who made or sold pallets.

variants.name: Pollitt
meanings: English (Lancashire): from the personal name Hippolytus, Greek Hippolytos, comprising elements meaning ‘horse’ + ‘to release’ (see Hyppolite ).

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Simon , now rare, but more common in the Middle Ages, when it gave rise to the surnames Simms and Simpson.

variants.name: Simon
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

nameMeanings.name: Alexander
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Paulett married Erenburga de Paulett before 1185, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Paulett a épousé Erenburga de Paulett avant 1185, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Paulett hat vor 1185 in England Erenburga de Paulett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Paulett lui sposato Erenburga de Paulett prima del 1185, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Paulettは1185年以前にイギリスでErenburga de Paulettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Paulett(은)는 1185년 이전 영국에서 Erenburga de Paulett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Paulett casou-se com Erenburga de Paulett antes 1185, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Paulett женился на женщине по имени Erenburga de Paulett перед 1185 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Alexander de Paulett se casó con Erenburga de Paulett antes de 1185, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija.
